get.description: Retrieves all login tokens associated to the current user.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

post.description: Creates a new login token to authenticate a user, for use in CMS integrations. Login tokens are valid for five minutes and can only be used once.

step.description: An edit step in the chart editor.

delete.description: Deletes an existing login token by the current user.

get.description: Login using a one-time login token and redirect to the URL associated with the token. For use in CMS integrations.

get.summary: Get list of valid token scopes.
get.description: Requires scope `auth:read`.
Response will not include full tokens for security reasons. Requires scope `auth:read`.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

post.description:
This endpoint will create a new API Token and show it in the response body.
                     It is possible to create a comment with every token to have a reference where it is used.
                     Make sure to save the token somewhere, since you won't be able to see it again.  Requires scope `auth:write`.

comment.description: The comment can be everything. Tip: Use something to remember where this specific token is used.

items.description: scopes to be granted for this token

delete.description: Delete (=revoke) an API access token. Check [/v3/auth/tokens](ref:authtokens) to get the IDs of your available tokens.  Requires scope `auth:write`.

parameters.description: ID of the token to be deleted.
parameters.name: id

put.description: Edit an API access token. Check [/v3/auth/tokens](ref:authtokens) to get the IDs of your available tokens.  Requires scope `auth:write`.

parameters.description: ID of the token to be edited.
parameters.name: id

comment.description: The comment can be everything. Tip: Use something to remember where this specific token is used.

items.description: scopes to be granted for this token

get.summary: Get list of all available basemaps
get.description: This endpoint fetches a list of available basemaps (metadata only)
This endpoint fetches a basemap's content and metadata.

parameters.description: Set to true if you want the basemap in WGS84
parameters.name: wgs84

get.summary: Fetch list of key values in a basemap
get.description: This endpoint fetches a list of all available values for a basemap key, e.g. https://api.datawrapper.de/v3/basemaps/world-2019/DW_NAME

Search and filter a list of your charts.
                        The returned chart objects, do not include the full chart metadata.
                        To get the full metadata add ?expand=true or use [/v3/charts/{id}](ref:getchartsid).  Requires scope `chart:read`.

x-alternatives.description: Search through visualizations created by the authenticated user
Search through visualizations created by the authenticated user

parameters.description: Flag to filter results by publish status
parameters.name: published

parameters.description: Search for charts with a specific title.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: List visualizations inside a specific folder

x-alternatives.description: List visualizations inside a specific folder

parameters.name: folderId

parameters.description: List visualizations belonging to a specific team. Use teamId=null to search for user visualizations not part of a team
parameters.name: teamId

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Filter visualizations by the last editor step they've been opened in (1=upload, 2=describe, 3=visualize, etc)
parameters.name: minLastEditStep

parameters.description: When set to true, the response includes additional properties such as visualization metadata.
parameters.name: expand

publicId.description: Public ID of the visualization. May be different from the internal ID, if *hash publishing* is enabled.

authorId.description: ID of user that created the visualization.

organizationId.description: ID of the team that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is private.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is in the root of a team, or your private archive.

theme.description: ID of theme applied to the visualization

createdAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last edited.

publishedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last published. `null`, if the visualization has not been published yet.

lastEditStep.description: A number encoding which of the editor steps this chart has been edited in so far. 5 = published.

publicVersion.description: Indicates how many times a visualization has been published.

name.description: Name of the user who created the visualization

email.description: Email address of the user who created the visualization

avatar.description: URL pointing to the user avatar

full.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image includes header & footer).

plain.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image is just the visualization, without header & footer).

url.description: API URL for the visualization, can be used to retreive additional information, including its metadata.

ids.description: The IDs of the charts that should be updated.

patch.description: An object containing the chart attributes that should be updated.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization should be moved into. The authenticated user must have access to this folder.

teamId.description: ID of the team that should own the visualization. The authenticated user must have access to this team.

deletedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was deleted.

forkable.description: Indicates if the visualization has been shared in the Datawrapper River.

title.description: Title of your visualization. This will be the visualization headline.

forkable.description: Set to true if you want to allow other users to fork this visualization

organizationId.description: ID of the team (formerly known as organization) that the visualization should be created in.  The authenticated user must have access to this team.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization should be created in. The authenticated user must have access to this folder.

lastEditStep.description: Current position in chart editor workflow

metadata.description: Metadata that saves all visualization specific settings and options.

axes.description: Mapping of dataset columns to visualization "axes"

byline.description: Byline as shown in the visualization footer

source-name.description: Source as shown in visualization footer

source-url.description: Source URL as shown in visualization footer

post.summary: Copies charts in place or into another folder

ids.description: The IDs of the charts that should be copied.

destination.description: Destination for the copied charts. If not provided, charts will be copied in place.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization should be duplicated into. The authenticated user must have access to this folder. Null is the root folder of user or team.

teamId.description: ID of the team that should own the visualization. The authenticated user must have access to this team. Null is the user's private archive.

markAsCopy.description: Determines whether "(Copy)" will be added to the title of the copied chart. Will be overriden if the source chart is a template owned by another team/user ("Edit in Datawrapper"), in which case "(Copy)" is never added.

get.description: Request an asset associated with a chart. Requires scope `chart:read`.

put.description:
Upload data for a chart, which is usually a CSV.
                        An example looks like this: `/v3/charts/{id}/assets/{id}.csv`. Requires scope `chart:write`.

requestBody.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.

x-alternatives.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.

get.summary: Gets all comments for a chart
get.description: Requires scope `chart:read`.

post.summary: Create a new comment for a chart

get.description: Request the data of a chart, which is usually a CSV. Requires scope `chart:read`.

put.description: Upload data for a chart or map. Requires scope `chart:write`.

requestBody.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.

x-alternatives.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.

post.summary: Updates a chart's external data source.
post.description: If a chart has an external data source configured, this endpoint fetches the data and saves it to the chart. Requires scope `chart:read`.

get.summary: Get share URLs for a chart
get.description: Request the available URLs to directly share a chart.

get.summary: Get embed codes for a chart
get.description: Request the responsive and static embed code of a chart. Requires scope `chart:read`.

Export your chart as image or document for use in print or presentations.
                        Not all formats might be available to you, based on your account. Requires scope `chart:read`.

post.description:
Export your chart as image or document for use in print or presentations. Returns the URL to query the async export status.
                        Not all formats might be available to you, based on your account. Requires scope `chart:read`.

get.description: After triggering an asynchronous export this route can be used to find out if the export has finished, yet. It returns either the HTTP code 425 to indicate that the export isn't ready or HTTP code 200 along with the export result.

post.description: This creates an editable copy of a reusable visualization from the [Datawrapper River](https://river.datawrapper.de/). Requires scope `chart:write`.

get.description: Check the publish status of a chart.  Requires scope `chart:read`.

post.description: This will remove all public files, including the embeds and published images. Requires scope `chart:write`.

get.description: Get a list of folders and their associated charts. Requires scope `folder:read`.

parameters.description: If present, the response will only include the number of charts in each folder. Otherwise the response includes the full chart information.
parameters.name: compact

teamId.description: The team that the folder belongs to. If `teamId` is empty, the folder will belong to the user directly.

parentId.description: The parent folder that the folder belongs to.

post.summary: Copy folders to a new destination folder/team/user.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the folders should be copied into. The authenticated user must have access to this folder. Null is the root folder of user or team.

teamId.description: ID of the team that owns the destination folder and will own the copied folders and charts. If folderId is provided the team owning this folder must match with this ID. The authenticated user must have access to this team. Null is the user's private archive.

folders.description: IDs of folders that should be moved to the new destination. Must be accessible by the authenticated user. Not more than 100 are allowed per request.

patch.summary: Move folders to a new destination folder/team/user.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the folders should be copied into. The authenticated user must have access to this folder. Null is the root folder of user or team.

teamId.description: ID of the team that owns the desination folder and will own the copied folders and charts. If folderId is provided the team owning this folder must match with this ID. The authenticated user must have access to this team. Null is the user's private archive.

folders.description: IDs of folders that should be moved to the new destination. Must be accessible by the authenticated user. Not more than 1000 are allowed per request.

delete.description: Deletes the specified folder. Requires scope `folder:write`.

get.description: Get a specific folder. Requires scope `folder:read`.

userId.description: If set, this is a private folder, and it belongs to the indicated user. If unset, the folder is located in a shared team archive (see `teamId`).

teamId.description: The ID of the team that this folder is in. If unset, this folder is private.

parentId.description: The id of the folder that this folder is in. If 'null', the folder is in the root of either a team, or your private charts. (See `userId` and `teamId`, to determine which)

children.description: List of top-level subfolders of this folder.

charts.description: List of visualizations in this folder. To access the visualizations in subfolders of this folder, those folders must be queried separately.

userId.description: The user this folder belongs to.

teamId.description: The team this folder belongs to.

teamId.description: The ID of the team that this folder is in. If unset, this folder is private.

parentId.description: The id of the folder that this folder is in. If 'null', the folder is in the root of either a team, or your private charts. (See `userId` and `teamId`, to determine which)

children.description: List of top-level subfolders of this folder.

charts.description: List of visualizations in this folder. To access the visualizations in subfolders of this folder, those folders must be queried separately.

children.description: List of top-level subfolders of this folder.

charts.description: List of visualizations in this folder. To access the visualizations in subfolders of this folder, those folders must be queried separately.

delete.description:
**Be careful!** This is a destructive action.
                        By deleting your account you will loose access to all of your charts.
                        If this endpoint should be used in an application (CMS), it is recommended to
                        ask the user for confirmation. Requires scope `user:write`.

email.description: User email address to confirm deletion.

get.description: Requires scope `user:read`.

role.description: Your new role. This can only be changed if you are an admin.

presenceColor.description: The preferred color to use for user precense pin.

avatar.description: `''` sets the avatar to the initial, while `null` sets avatar to custom_avatar.

get.description: Retreive embedding information about a Datawrapper visualization. See our [oEmbed tutorial](https://developer.datawrapper.de/docs/embedding-charts-via-oembed) and the official [oEmbed specification](https://oembed.com/) for more information.

parameters.description: The response format. Currenly only JSON is supported.

parameters.name: url

parameters.description: The maximum width of the embedded visualization
parameters.name: maxwidth

parameters.description: The maximum height of the embedded visualization
parameters.name: maxheight

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

delete.description: **Be careful!** This is a destructive action that can only be performed by team owners. Requires scope `team:write`.

parameters.description: ID of the team to delete.
parameters.name: id

get.description: Requires scope `team:read` or `team:write`.

parameters.description: ID of the team to fetch.
parameters.name: id

get.description: Get a list of team members and some additional information like their team role. Requires scopes `team:read` *and* `user:read`.

parameters.description: ID of the team to fetch members for.
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Search for a team name or id including this term.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: ID of the team member to remove from team.
parameters.name: userId

parameters.description: ID of the team member you want to change the status of.
parameters.name: userId

get.description:
Get a list of themes accessible by the authenticated user
                        The returned theme objects do not include the full theme configuration.
                        Requires scope `chart:read`.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

get.description: Requires scope `user:read`.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

delete.description:
**Be careful!** This is a destructive action.
                        By deleting your account you will loose access to all of your charts.
                        If this endpoint should be used in an application (CMS), it is recommended to
                        ask the user for confirmation. Requires scope `user:write`.

email.description: User email address to confirm deletion.

get.description: Requires scope `user:read`.

role.description: New user role. Can only be changed by admins.

activateToken.description: Activate token, typically used to unset it when activating user.

presenceColor.description: The preferred color to use for user precense pin.

avatar.description: `''` sets the avatar to fallback, while `null` sets avatar to custom_avatar.

get.summary: Get a list of recently edited charts
get.description: Requires scopes `user:read` *and* `chart:read`.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Filter visualizations by the last editor step they've been opened in (1=upload, 2=describe, 3=visualize, etc)
parameters.name: minLastEditStep

publicId.description: Public ID of the visualization. May be different from the internal ID, if *hash publishing* is enabled.

authorId.description: ID of user that created the visualization.

organizationId.description: ID of the team that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is private.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is in the root of a team, or your private archive.

theme.description: ID of theme applied to the visualization

createdAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last edited.

publishedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last published. `null`, if the visualization has not been published yet.

lastEditStep.description: A number encoding which of the editor steps this chart has been edited in so far. 5 = published.

publicVersion.description: Indicates how many times a visualization has been published.

name.description: Name of the user who created the visualization

email.description: Email address of the user who created the visualization

avatar.description: URL pointing to the user avatar

full.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image includes header & footer).

plain.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image is just the visualization, without header & footer).

url.description: API URL for the visualization, can be used to retreive additional information, including its metadata.

get.summary: Get a list of recently published charts
get.description: Requires scopes `user:read` *and* `chart:read`.

parameters.description: Maximum items to fetch. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip. Useful for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Filter visualizations by the last editor step they've been opened in (1=upload, 2=describe, 3=visualize, etc)
parameters.name: minLastEditStep

publicId.description: Public ID of the visualization. May be different from the internal ID, if *hash publishing* is enabled.

authorId.description: ID of user that created the visualization.

organizationId.description: ID of the team that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is private.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization is located in. If `null`, visualization is in the root of a team, or your private archive.

theme.description: ID of theme applied to the visualization

createdAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last edited.

publishedAt.description: Time and date when the visualization was last published. `null`, if the visualization has not been published yet.

lastEditStep.description: A number encoding which of the editor steps this chart has been edited in so far. 5 = published.

publicVersion.description: Indicates how many times a visualization has been published.

name.description: Name of the user who created the visualization

email.description: Email address of the user who created the visualization

avatar.description: URL pointing to the user avatar

full.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image includes header & footer).

plain.description: URL pointing to the most recently generated preview thumbnail for the visualization. (Image is just the visualization, without header & footer).

url.description: API URL for the visualization, can be used to retreive additional information, including its metadata.

activeTeam.description: The active team for the user

comment.description: The comment can be everything. Tip: Use something to remember where this specific token is used.

items.description: scopes to be granted for this token

title.description: Title of your chart. This will be the chart headline.

lastEditStep.description: Current position in chart editor workflow

metadata.description: Metadata that saves all chart specific settings and options.

organizationId.description: ID of the team (formerly known as organization) that the visualization should be created in.  The authenticated user must have access to this team.

folderId.description: ID of the folder that the visualization should be created in. The authenticated user must have access to this folder.

putChartsIdAssetsAssetBody.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.

x-alternatives.description: An asset used by the chart such as CSV data or custom JSON map.
